config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. In my view, society matters a lot, but it should not automatically come before the rights of the individual, because individual rights are what protect people from abuse by governments and majorities. In Indonesia, I have seen how easily “public interest” can be used as an excuse to silence criticism or overlook unfair treatment, so I am cautious about giving society priority by default. A fair society should balance both, but the individual should not be sacrificed too quickly.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari perspektif saya di Amerika Serikat, sejarah menunjukkan bahwa ketika “kepentingan umum” diberi prioritas terlalu besar, itu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pelanggaran kebebasan berbicara, privasi, dan perlakuan adil terhadap minoritas. Kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi justru masyarakat yang sehat bergantung pada hak individu yang kuat sebagai batas terhadap mayoritas dan pemerintah. Jadi, menurut saya, prioritasnya harus seimbang, dengan hak individu sebagai garis perlindungan utama, bukan sesuatu yang mudah dikorbankan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the idea that society should automatically come before the individual, because that is exactly how abuse gets dressed up as virtue. In Indonesia, the language of “public order” or “common good” is often used to pressure people into silence, and I have seen how minorities or critics can be treated as inconveniences rather than citizens with rights. Society is important, but it is not morally cleaner than the individual; if anything, a just society is one that protects individuals even when doing so is unpopular. So I would keep individual rights as the main safeguard, with social interests considered only when they do not crush basic freedoms.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena, dari sudut pandang saya di Amerika Serikat, memberi prioritas otomatis pada kepentingan masyarakat terlalu mudah berubah menjadi pembenaran untuk menekan kebebasan berbicara, privasi, dan hak minoritas. Argumen tentang “ketertiban umum” atau “kepentingan bersama” memang terdengar mulia, tetapi sejarah menunjukkan bahwa pemerintah dan mayoritas sering memakainya untuk melangkahi orang yang lebih lemah. Masyarakat yang benar-benar sehat justru bergantung pada hak individu yang kuat sebagai batas terhadap kekuasaan kolektif. Jadi bagi saya, kepentingan sosial penting, tetapi tidak boleh menjadi alasan utama untuk mengorbankan hak dasar individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I reject the idea that society should automatically outrank the individual, because in practice that often becomes a convenient excuse for authorities or majorities to silence dissent. From my Indonesian perspective, I have seen how words like "public order" and "common interest" can be used to pressure minorities, so I do not trust society to be morally safer than the individual by default. Social interests matter, of course, but they should be limited by clear rights that protect people from abuse, not used to erase those rights. A good society is one that can defend the common good without treating individuals as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu secara umum, karena menurut saya di Amerika Serikat itu terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan oleh pemerintah atau mayoritas. Argumen tentang ketertiban umum memang terdengar masuk akal, tetapi sejarah juga menunjukkan bahwa kebebasan berbicara, privasi, dan perlindungan minoritas justru paling rentan dikorbankan ketika “kepentingan bersama” dibuat terlalu dominan. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling cepat mengalahkan individu, melainkan yang mampu mencapai tujuan kolektif tanpa merendahkan hak dasar orang. Jadi saya melihat hak individu sebagai batas utama, bukan penghalang yang mudah disingkirkan demi kepentingan sosial.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
